attributes.description:
Controls the outer margin (spacing) around modal dialogs (overlay windows that appear on top of the main content). This creates breathing room around the dialog container. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-margin

Controls the stacking order of modal dialogs (determines which elements appear on top when multiple elements overlap). Higher values make the dialog appear above other elements. This ensures modals are always visible above page content.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-z-index

Controls the minimum width of modal dialogs. This ensures the dialog is always wide enough to be visible and properly displayed. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-min-width

Controls the maximum height of modal dialogs. This prevents the dialog from becoming too tall and ensures it fits within the viewport. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-max-height

Sets the background color of modal dialogs (overlay windows that appear on top of the main content). This is the main background color that appears behind the dialog content.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-background

Controls the corner rounding of modal dialogs. This makes the dialogs appear with rounded corners for a softer appearance.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-radius

Controls the internal spacing inside modal dialog headers (the top section that contains the title and close button). This creates breathing room around the header content. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-header-padding

Controls the thickness of the border at the bottom of modal dialog headers. This determines how thick the separator line appears between the header and body. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-header-border-width

Controls the style of the border at the bottom of modal dialog headers. 'solid' creates a continuous line, 'dashed' creates a dotted line, 'dotted' creates small dots.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-header-border-style

Sets the color of the border at the bottom of modal dialog headers. This determines the color of the separator line between the header and body.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-header-border-color

Controls the outer margin (spacing) around the modal dialog close button. This creates spacing around the close button. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-header-close-btn-margin

Controls the thickness of the border around the modal dialog close button. When set to 0, no border is visible. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-header-close-btn-border-width

Controls the size of the close button icon (the X or close symbol that appears in the modal dialog header). This affects how large the close icon appears. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-header-close-btn-font-size

Sets the color of the close button icon in modal dialog headers. This determines what color the close icon appears in.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-header-close-btn-color

Controls the spacing between modal dialog title elements (the gap between the title text and adjacent elements like icons). This creates breathing room around the title. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-title-gap

Controls the size of modal dialog title text (the heading text that appears at the top of modal dialogs). This affects how large the title text appears.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-title-font-size

Sets the typeface (font style) for modal dialog title text. This determines whether the title text appears in Arial, Times New Roman, or other font styles.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-title-font-family

Controls how thick or bold modal dialog title text appears. 'normal' makes it regular weight, 'bold' makes it thicker, and numbers (100-900) provide fine control over thickness.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-title-font-weight

Controls the vertical spacing between lines when modal dialog title text wraps to multiple lines. This ensures proper spacing for the title text.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-title-line-height

Controls the horizontal spacing between individual letters in modal dialog title text. Positive values spread letters apart, negative values bring them closer together.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-title-letter-spacing

Controls the internal spacing inside modal dialog body sections (the main content area of the dialog). This creates breathing room around the body content. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-body-padding

Controls the internal spacing inside modal dialog footers (the bottom section that contains action buttons). This creates breathing room around the footer content. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-footer-padding

Controls the spacing between footer buttons in modal dialogs (the gap between action buttons in the footer). This creates breathing room between buttons. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-footer-gap

Controls the vertical padding (top and bottom spacing) inside footer buttons in modal dialogs. This creates breathing room above and below the button text. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-footer-btn-padding-vertical

Controls the horizontal padding (left and right spacing) inside footer buttons in modal dialogs. This creates breathing room on the left and right sides of the button text. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-footer-btn-padding-horizontal

Controls the size of modal dialog icons (icons that appear in the dialog to indicate the dialog type or provide visual context). This affects how large the icons appear.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-icon-font-size

Controls how thick or bold modal dialog icons appear. This affects the visual weight of the icons.
CSS variable: --wm-modal-icon-font-weight
